respiratory tract-Neutrophil.organ_specimen: Respiratory tract, sputum, bronchial brushing, or lavage specimen
respiratory tract-Neutrophil.diagnostic_code: PSC Category II: Negative — acute neutrophilic inflammation
respiratory tract-Neutrophil.cytomorphology: Singles and loose aggregates in mucus show segmented multilobed nuclei with dense clumped chromatin, inconspicuous nucleoli, high N:C ratio, and scant pale finely granular cytoplasm.
respiratory tract-Neutrophil.background_and_immunoprofile: Mucopurulent exudate, fibrin and bacteria; CD15, CD66b, myeloperoxidase positive; cytokeratin negative.
respiratory tract-Neutrophil.key_distinguishing_feature: Multilobed segmented nucleus distinguishes this vs Lymphocyte, which has a single round dense nucleus and thin basophilic cytoplasmic rim.

respiratory tract-Alveolar macrophages.organ_specimen: Respiratory tract, sputum or bronchoalveolar lavage specimen
respiratory tract-Alveolar macrophages.diagnostic_code: PSC Category II: Negative — alveolar macrophages
respiratory tract-Alveolar macrophages.cytomorphology: Singly dispersed or loose clusters of histiocytes show eccentric oval or bean-shaped nuclei, fine chromatin, inconspicuous nucleoli, low N:C ratio, and abundant foamy vacuolated cytoplasm with carbon or hemosiderin.
respiratory tract-Alveolar macrophages.background_and_immunoprofile: Watery mucus with dust, hemosiderin or debris; CD68, CD163 positive; epithelial markers negative.
respiratory tract-Alveolar macrophages.key_distinguishing_feature: Pigment-laden foamy cytoplasm distinguishes this vs Adenocarcinoma, which forms glandular clusters with malignant nucleoli and mucin vacuoles.

respiratory tract-Ciliated columnar epit.organ_specimen: Respiratory tract, bronchial brushing or washing specimen
respiratory tract-Ciliated columnar epit.diagnostic_code: PSC Category II: Negative — benign bronchial epithelium
respiratory tract-Ciliated columnar epit.cytomorphology: Cohesive sheets, strips, and picket-fence groups show basally oriented uniform oval nuclei, smooth membranes, fine chromatin, low-to-moderate N:C ratio, and delicate basophilic columnar cytoplasm with apical cilia.
respiratory tract-Ciliated columnar epit.background_and_immunoprofile: Clean mucus with goblet cells; CK7, TTF-1 variably positive; p40 limited to basal cells.
respiratory tract-Ciliated columnar epit.key_distinguishing_feature: Apical cilia and terminal bars distinguish this vs Adenocarcinoma, which lacks cilia and forms disordered three-dimensional glandular clusters.

respiratory tract-Lymphocyte.organ_specimen: Respiratory tract, bronchoalveolar lavage or lymph-node FNA specimen
respiratory tract-Lymphocyte.diagnostic_code: PSC Category II: Negative — benign lymphoid cells
respiratory tract-Lymphocyte.cytomorphology: Small dispersed mononuclear cells show round uniform nuclei with dense clumped mature chromatin, absent nucleoli, very high N:C ratio, and a scant thin basophilic cytoplasmic rim.
respiratory tract-Lymphocyte.background_and_immunoprofile: Reactive lymphoid background; CD45 positive with mixed CD3 T cells and CD20 B cells.
respiratory tract-Lymphocyte.key_distinguishing_feature: Uniform small nuclei without molding distinguish this vs Small cell carcinoma, which shows nuclear molding, crush artifact, and neuroendocrine chromatin.

respiratory tract-Impurity.organ_specimen: Respiratory specimen, smear or liquid-based cytology preparation
respiratory tract-Impurity.diagnostic_code: PSC Category I: Nondiagnostic — contaminant or artifact
respiratory tract-Impurity.cytomorphology: Amorphous granular, filamentous, refractile, or angular noncellular material lacks true cellular arrangement, nuclear membranes, chromatin, nucleoli, N:C relationship, and definable cytoplasmic borders.
respiratory tract-Impurity.background_and_immunoprofile: Acellular mucus, stain precipitate, fibers, talc or lubricant; IHC noncontributory, no cytokeratin/CD45/CD68 staining.
respiratory tract-Impurity.key_distinguishing_feature: Complete absence of nuclear membrane and cytoplasm distinguishes this vs Macrophages, which show true foamy cytoplasm and eccentric nuclei.

respiratory tract-Squamous epithelial ce.organ_specimen: Respiratory tract, sputum or bronchial brushing specimen
respiratory tract-Squamous epithelial ce.diagnostic_code: PSC Category II: Negative — benign squamous epithelium
respiratory tract-Squamous epithelial ce.cytomorphology: Flat sheets and isolated polygonal cells show small central or pyknotic nuclei, smooth contours, condensed chromatin, low N:C ratio, and abundant dense cyanophilic, eosinophilic, or orangeophilic cytoplasm.
respiratory tract-Squamous epithelial ce.background_and_immunoprofile: Upper-airway contamination or metaplasia; p40, p63, CK5/6 positive; Ki-67 low.
respiratory tract-Squamous epithelial ce.key_distinguishing_feature: Bland pyknotic nuclei with abundant sharp-bordered cytoplasm distinguish this vs Squamous cell carcinoma, which shows tumor diathesis and pleomorphic keratinization.

respiratory tract-Diseased cells.organ_specimen: Respiratory tract, bronchial brushing, washing, or FNA specimen
respiratory tract-Diseased cells.diagnostic_code: PSC Category III: Atypical — reactive/indeterminate epithelial atypia
respiratory tract-Diseased cells.cytomorphology: Scattered cells or loose clusters show partial loss of polarity, cytomegaly, mild anisonucleosis, smooth-to-slightly irregular membranes, fine-to-mildly coarse chromatin, and vacuolated or metaplastic cytoplasm.
respiratory tract-Diseased cells.background_and_immunoprofile: Inflammation, necrotic debris, therapy effect or infection; lineage CK7/p40/TTF-1 retained, Ki-67 not high.
respiratory tract-Diseased cells.key_distinguishing_feature: Limited mild anisonucleosis with retained lineage pattern distinguishes this vs PSC Category V Suspicious, which has quantitatively stronger malignant atypia.

Serous effusion-Negative samples.organ_specimen: Serous cavity, pleural/peritoneal/pericardial effusion cytology specimen
Serous effusion-Negative samples.diagnostic_code: TIS Category II: NFM — negative for malignancy
Serous effusion-Negative samples.cytomorphology: Benign mesothelial cells occur singly, in flat sheets, or small windows-bearing groups with round uniform nuclei, fine chromatin, occasional nucleoli, low N:C ratio, and dense two-tone cytoplasm.
Serous effusion-Negative samples.background_and_immunoprofile: Proteinaceous fluid with lymphocytes/macrophages; calretinin, WT1, D2-40 positive; claudin-4, BerEP4 negative.
Serous effusion-Negative samples.key_distinguishing_feature: Intercellular windows and smooth even nuclei distinguish this vs Metastatic adenocarcinoma, which forms tight morules with claudin-4 positivity.

Serous effusion-Diseased cells.organ_specimen: Serous cavity, pleural/peritoneal/pericardial effusion cytology specimen
Serous effusion-Diseased cells.diagnostic_code: TIS Category III: AUS — atypia of undetermined significance
Serous effusion-Diseased cells.cytomorphology: Reactive clusters or limited papillary/pseudoglandular groups show karyomegaly, mild chromatin irregularity, smooth-to-slightly irregular membranes, prominent nucleoli, and abundant dense or vacuolated mesothelial cytoplasm.
Serous effusion-Diseased cells.background_and_immunoprofile: Serositis with blood or inflammation; mesothelial calretinin/WT1 positive; claudin-4 absent or equivocal.
Serous effusion-Diseased cells.key_distinguishing_feature: Mild karyomegaly with mesothelial windows distinguishes this vs Malignant effusion, which shows tight three-dimensional epithelial clusters and claudin-4 positivity.

Thyroid gland-PTC.organ_specimen: Thyroid gland, ultrasound-guided fine-needle aspiration specimen
Thyroid gland-PTC.diagnostic_code: Bethesda VI: Malignant — papillary thyroid carcinoma
Thyroid gland-PTC.cytomorphology: Papillary fragments, syncytial sheets, and micropapillae show enlarged oval overlapping nuclei, powdery chromatin, grooves, intranuclear pseudoinclusions, high N:C ratio, and delicate cyanophilic cytoplasm.
Thyroid gland-PTC.background_and_immunoprofile: Scant colloid, psammoma bodies, giant cells; CK19, HBME-1, galectin-3, TTF-1, PAX8 positive.
Thyroid gland-PTC.key_distinguishing_feature: True intranuclear pseudoinclusions with grooves distinguish this vs Follicular neoplasm, which lacks diffuse PTC nuclear membrane abnormalities.

Thyroid gland-SPTC.organ_specimen: Thyroid gland, ultrasound-guided fine-needle aspiration specimen
Thyroid gland-SPTC.diagnostic_code: Bethesda V: Suspicious for malignancy — suspicious PTC
Thyroid gland-SPTC.cytomorphology: Limited cellularity with papillaroid clusters or crowded sheets shows focal enlarged oval nuclei, occasional grooves or pseudoinclusions, pale chromatin, increased N:C ratio, and scant delicate cytoplasm.
Thyroid gland-SPTC.background_and_immunoprofile: Scant colloid; CK19, HBME-1, galectin-3 may support PTC; BRAF VE1 optional.
Thyroid gland-SPTC.key_distinguishing_feature: Focal PTC-type nuclear changes distinguish this vs Bethesda VI PTC, which requires a quantitatively convincing malignant follicular-cell population.

Thyroid gland-NS.organ_specimen: Thyroid gland, ultrasound-guided fine-needle aspiration specimen
Thyroid gland-NS.diagnostic_code: Bethesda I: Nondiagnostic — scant follicular cells
Thyroid gland-NS.cytomorphology: Blood, cyst fluid, macrophages, or colloid predominate with absent or too few follicular cell groups; nuclei are absent or bland, and cytoplasm is foamy, degenerative, or obscured.
Thyroid gland-NS.background_and_immunoprofile: Cyst fluid, blood, clotting or drying artifact; IHC not informative; thyroglobulin highlights rare follicular cells.
Thyroid gland-NS.key_distinguishing_feature: Insufficient benign follicular groups distinguish this vs Bethesda II Benign, which shows adequate colloid-associated follicular epithelium.

Thyroid gland-Macrophages.organ_specimen: Thyroid gland, cyst-fluid fine-needle aspiration specimen
Thyroid gland-Macrophages.diagnostic_code: Bethesda I: Nondiagnostic — cyst fluid only
Thyroid gland-Macrophages.cytomorphology: Singly dispersed or loose histiocytic clusters show eccentric oval nuclei, smooth membranes, fine chromatin, inconspicuous nucleoli, low N:C ratio, and abundant foamy vacuolated cytoplasm with hemosiderin.
Thyroid gland-Macrophages.background_and_immunoprofile: Cyst fluid, old hemorrhage and colloid; CD68/CD163 positive; thyroglobulin, TTF-1 negative.
Thyroid gland-Macrophages.key_distinguishing_feature: Foamy hemosiderin-laden cytoplasm distinguishes this vs Follicular epithelial cells, which form cohesive sheets and express thyroglobulin.

Thyroid gland-AUC.organ_specimen: Thyroid gland, ultrasound-guided fine-needle aspiration specimen
Thyroid gland-AUC.diagnostic_code: Bethesda III: AUS — atypia of undetermined significance
Thyroid gland-AUC.cytomorphology: Sparsely cellular microfollicles or crowded trabecular groups show mild nuclear enlargement, pale chromatin, rare grooves, slight membrane irregularity, and scant-to-moderate cytoplasm, sometimes oncocytic granular.
Thyroid gland-AUC.background_and_immunoprofile: Scant colloid or Hürthle-cell change; molecular testing optional; CK19/HBME-1 not definitively diagnostic.
Thyroid gland-AUC.key_distinguishing_feature: Rare grooves or limited microfollicles distinguish this vs Bethesda IV Follicular neoplasm, which shows a predominant microfollicular pattern.

Thyroid gland-Negative samples.organ_specimen: Thyroid gland, ultrasound-guided fine-needle aspiration specimen
Thyroid gland-Negative samples.diagnostic_code: Bethesda II: Benign — follicular nodular disease
Thyroid gland-Negative samples.cytomorphology: Flat honeycomb sheets and macrofollicular groups show uniform round-to-oval nuclei, smooth membranes, fine granular chromatin, inconspicuous nucleoli, low N:C ratio, and moderate delicate cytoplasm.
Thyroid gland-Negative samples.background_and_immunoprofile: Abundant watery or thick colloid, macrophages possible; thyroglobulin, TTF-1, PAX8 positive in follicular cells.
Thyroid gland-Negative samples.key_distinguishing_feature: Abundant colloid with honeycomb sheets distinguishes this vs Follicular neoplasm, which is cellular and microfollicular with scant colloid.

Thyroid gland-FC.organ_specimen: Thyroid gland, fine-needle aspiration smear specimen
Thyroid gland-FC.diagnostic_code: Bethesda II: Benign — follicular epithelial cells
Thyroid gland-FC.cytomorphology: Cohesive monolayer sheets, honeycomb groups, and small follicles show round uniform centrally placed nuclei, smooth membranes, fine chromatin, low-to-moderate N:C ratio, and delicate pale cytoplasm.
Thyroid gland-FC.background_and_immunoprofile: Colloid-rich thyroid aspirate; follicular cells express thyroglobulin, TTF-1, PAX8; calcitonin negative.
Thyroid gland-FC.key_distinguishing_feature: Orderly honeycomb follicular architecture distinguishes this vs Papillary thyroid carcinoma, which shows crowding, grooves, pseudoinclusions, and powdery chromatin.

Urine-NHGUC.organ_specimen: Urinary tract, voided urine or bladder washing specimen
Urine-NHGUC.diagnostic_code: Paris II: NHGUC — negative for high-grade urothelial carcinoma
Urine-NHGUC.cytomorphology: Benign urothelial cells, umbrella cells, and squamous cells form flat sheets or small clusters with smooth oval nuclei, fine chromatin, inconspicuous nucleoli, low N:C ratio, and abundant cytoplasm.
Urine-NHGUC.background_and_immunoprofile: Urine matrix with inflammation/instrumentation; GATA3, uroplakin positive; CK20 limited, p53 wild-type, Ki-67 low.
Urine-NHGUC.key_distinguishing_feature: Low N:C ratio with smooth chromatin distinguishes this vs AUC, which requires increased N:C ratio and hyperchromatic irregular nuclei.

Urine-SHGUC.organ_specimen: Urinary tract, voided urine or bladder washing specimen
Urine-SHGUC.diagnostic_code: Paris IV: SHGUC — suspicious for high-grade urothelial carcinoma
Urine-SHGUC.cytomorphology: Few severely atypical urothelial cells occur singly or in small clusters with high N:C ratio above 0.7, hyperchromasia, coarse chromatin, irregular nuclear contours, and scant dense cytoplasm.
Urine-SHGUC.background_and_immunoprofile: Hematuric or necrotic urine; CK20 diffuse, p53 aberrant, Ki-67 high may support HGUC.
Urine-SHGUC.key_distinguishing_feature: Few high-grade cells distinguish this vs HGUC, which requires many similarly abnormal cells with convincing high-grade morphology.

Urine-AUC.organ_specimen: Urinary tract, voided urine or bladder washing specimen
Urine-AUC.diagnostic_code: Paris III: AUC — atypical urothelial cells
Urine-AUC.cytomorphology: Scattered urothelial cells or small groups show increased N:C ratio above 0.5, mild hyperchromasia, irregular nuclear contours or clumpy chromatin, and scant-to-moderate cytoplasm.
Urine-AUC.background_and_immunoprofile: Inflammation, stones, instrumentation or therapy effect common; CK20/p53/Ki-67 adjunctive but not definitive.
Urine-AUC.key_distinguishing_feature: Increased N:C ratio with only one high-grade criterion distinguishes this vs SHGUC, which has high N:C ratio and multiple severe features.

Urine-HGUC.organ_specimen: Urinary tract, voided urine or bladder washing specimen
Urine-HGUC.diagnostic_code: Paris V: HGUC — high-grade urothelial carcinoma
Urine-HGUC.cytomorphology: Many dyscohesive cells and irregular clusters show N:C ratio above 0.7, marked hyperchromasia, coarse clumpy chromatin, irregular nuclear membranes, occasional nucleoli, and scant dense cytoplasm.
Urine-HGUC.background_and_immunoprofile: Necrotic or bloody urine; GATA3, p63, uroplakin positive; CK20 diffuse, p53 aberrant, Ki-67 high.
Urine-HGUC.key_distinguishing_feature: Numerous cells meeting high N:C and severe nuclear criteria distinguish this vs SHGUC, which has too few diagnostic cells.

TCT_CCD-normal.organ_specimen: Uterine cervix, liquid-based ThinPrep cervical cytology specimen
TCT_CCD-normal.diagnostic_code: Bethesda NILM: negative for intraepithelial lesion or malignancy
TCT_CCD-normal.cytomorphology: Superficial and intermediate squamous cells form flat sheets with small pyknotic or vesicular smooth nuclei, fine chromatin, low N:C ratio, and abundant transparent cyanophilic cytoplasm.
TCT_CCD-normal.background_and_immunoprofile: Clean mucus with lactobacilli; p16 negative/non-block, Ki-67 limited to basal metaplastic cells.
TCT_CCD-normal.key_distinguishing_feature: Low N:C ratio and smooth nuclei distinguish this vs ASC-US, which shows nuclear enlargement beyond reactive expectations.

TCT_CCD-ascus.organ_specimen: Uterine cervix, liquid-based ThinPrep cervical cytology specimen
TCT_CCD-ascus.diagnostic_code: Bethesda ASC-US: atypical squamous cells, undetermined significance
TCT_CCD-ascus.cytomorphology: Mature squamous cells occur singly or in sheets with nuclear enlargement about 2.5–3 times normal, mild hyperchromasia, slight membrane irregularity, and abundant cytoplasm without definitive koilocytosis.
TCT_CCD-ascus.background_and_immunoprofile: Inflammation or atrophy may coexist; hrHPV triage recommended; p16/Ki-67 dual stain may help.
TCT_CCD-ascus.key_distinguishing_feature: Borderline nuclear enlargement without true koilocytosis distinguishes this vs LSIL, which has sharply defined perinuclear halos and irregular hyperchromatic nuclei.

TCT_CCD-asch.organ_specimen: Uterine cervix, liquid-based ThinPrep cervical cytology specimen
TCT_CCD-asch.diagnostic_code: Bethesda ASC-H: atypical squamous cells, cannot exclude HSIL
TCT_CCD-asch.cytomorphology: Small metaplastic-like cells in singles or crowded fragments show high N:C ratio, hyperchromatic irregular nuclei, coarse chromatin, absent nucleoli, and scant dense cyanophilic cytoplasm.
TCT_CCD-asch.background_and_immunoprofile: Atrophy or metaplasia may confound; block p16 and increased Ki-67 support HSIL.
TCT_CCD-asch.key_distinguishing_feature: Focal high N:C atypical cells distinguish this vs HSIL, where abnormal immature squamous cells are quantitatively sufficient.

TCT_CCD-lsil.organ_specimen: Uterine cervix, liquid-based ThinPrep cervical cytology specimen
TCT_CCD-lsil.diagnostic_code: Bethesda LSIL: low-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion
TCT_CCD-lsil.cytomorphology: Mature superficial/intermediate squamous cells show enlarged hyperchromatic irregular nuclei at least three times normal, binucleation, coarse chromatin, low-to-moderate N:C ratio, and abundant cytoplasm with koilocytotic halos.
TCT_CCD-lsil.background_and_immunoprofile: Koilocytic inflammatory background; HPV effect present, p16 variable/patchy, Ki-67 mildly expanded.
TCT_CCD-lsil.key_distinguishing_feature: Koilocytotic halo with nuclear atypia distinguishes this vs ASC-US, which lacks definitive HPV cytopathic nuclear changes.

TCT_CCD-hsil_scc_omn.organ_specimen: Uterine cervix, liquid-based ThinPrep cervical cytology specimen
TCT_CCD-hsil_scc_omn.diagnostic_code: Bethesda HSIL/SCC: high-grade SIL or squamous carcinoma
TCT_CCD-hsil_scc_omn.cytomorphology: Small immature cells in syncytial aggregates or singles show very high N:C ratio, hyperchromatic coarse chromatin, angular irregular nuclei, absent or macronucleoli, and scant basophilic or keratinized cytoplasm.
TCT_CCD-hsil_scc_omn.background_and_immunoprofile: Tumor diathesis if invasive; p16 block positive, Ki-67 high, p40/p63 positive in SCC.
TCT_CCD-hsil_scc_omn.key_distinguishing_feature: Immature high N:C cells with block p16 distinguish this vs LSIL, which has mature koilocytes and lower N:C ratio.

TCT_CCD-agc_adenocarcinoma_em.organ_specimen: Uterine cervix, liquid-based cervical cytology specimen
TCT_CCD-agc_adenocarcinoma_em.diagnostic_code: Bethesda AGC/AIS: atypical glandular cells or adenocarcinoma
TCT_CCD-agc_adenocarcinoma_em.cytomorphology: Endocervical strips, rosettes, feathered sheets, or endometrial ball-like clusters show enlarged oval pseudostratified nuclei, coarse chromatin, nucleoli, high N:C ratio, and mucinous or scant cyanophilic cytoplasm.
TCT_CCD-agc_adenocarcinoma_em.background_and_immunoprofile: Mucin, blood or necrosis; p16/CEA support endocervical, ER/PAX8/vimentin support endometrial origin.
TCT_CCD-agc_adenocarcinoma_em.key_distinguishing_feature: Feathering and rosettes distinguish this vs Reactive endocervical cells, which retain honeycomb polarity and smooth evenly spaced nuclei.

TCT_CCD-vaginalis.organ_specimen: Female lower genital tract, cervical-vaginal cytology specimen
TCT_CCD-vaginalis.diagnostic_code: Bethesda NILM: Trichomonas vaginalis organism present
TCT_CCD-vaginalis.cytomorphology: Pear-shaped or oval organisms lie singly among squamous cells with a small eccentric vesicular nucleus, fine chromatin, and pale finely granular cytoplasm; flagella or undulating membrane may be visible.
TCT_CCD-vaginalis.background_and_immunoprofile: Neutrophilic inflammatory background and reduced lactobacilli; IHC not routine, NAAT confirms infection.
TCT_CCD-vaginalis.key_distinguishing_feature: Eccentric nucleus with flagellated granular cytoplasm distinguishes this vs Neutrophil, which has multilobed nuclei and no organismal cytoplasm.

TCT_CCD-monilia.organ_specimen: Female lower genital tract, cervical-vaginal cytology specimen
TCT_CCD-monilia.diagnostic_code: Bethesda NILM: fungal organisms consistent with Candida
TCT_CCD-monilia.cytomorphology: Budding yeasts and elongated pseudohyphae intertwine with squamous cells; host nuclei are bland or mildly reactive, while gray-brown eosinophilic fungal cytoplasm creates spearing or shish-kebab arrays.
TCT_CCD-monilia.background_and_immunoprofile: Neutrophilic background; PAS/GMS stains highlight yeasts and pseudohyphae; IHC is not routinely used.
TCT_CCD-monilia.key_distinguishing_feature: Constricted pseudohyphae with budding yeasts distinguish this vs Trichomonas, which shows pear-shaped organisms with eccentric nuclei.

TCT_CCD-dysbacteriosis_herpes_act.organ_specimen: Female lower genital tract, cervical-vaginal cytology specimen
TCT_CCD-dysbacteriosis_herpes_act.diagnostic_code: Bethesda NILM: bacterial vaginosis, HSV, or Actinomyces
TCT_CCD-dysbacteriosis_herpes_act.cytomorphology: Clue cells, multinucleated HSV-infected cells, or cotton-ball filamentous colonies show normal BV nuclei or HSV ground-glass molded nuclei with margination, and cytoplasm coated by coccobacilli or inclusions.
TCT_CCD-dysbacteriosis_herpes_act.background_and_immunoprofile: Coccobacillary flora, acute inflammation or IUD-associated filaments; HSV IHC positive if needed; Gram/GMS helpful.
TCT_CCD-dysbacteriosis_herpes_act.key_distinguishing_feature: Clue-cell coating, HSV 3M nuclei, or Actinomyces filaments distinguish this vs Candida, which shows budding yeasts and pseudohyphae.

TCT_CCD-ec.organ_specimen: Uterine cervix, endocervical liquid-based cytology specimen
TCT_CCD-ec.diagnostic_code: Bethesda NILM: benign endocervical component present
TCT_CCD-ec.cytomorphology: Benign endocervical cells form honeycomb sheets, strips, and palisaded picket-fence groups with basally placed oval nuclei, fine chromatin, inconspicuous nucleoli, and mucinous columnar cytoplasm.
TCT_CCD-ec.background_and_immunoprofile: Clear mucus background; CK7, PAX8, CEA positive endocervical phenotype; p16 non-block.
TCT_CCD-ec.key_distinguishing_feature: Honeycomb polarity and picket-fence strips distinguish this vs AGC, which shows crowding, pseudostratification, feathering, and nuclear enlargement.

respiratory tract-Squamous cell carcinom.organ_specimen: Respiratory tract, bronchial brushing or lung FNA specimen
respiratory tract-Squamous cell carcinom.diagnostic_code: PSC Category VI: Malignant — squamous cell carcinoma
respiratory tract-Squamous cell carcinom.cytomorphology: Syncytial sheets and isolated keratinized cells show pleomorphic hyperchromatic irregular nuclei, coarse chromatin, variable nucleoli, high N:C ratio, dense orangeophilic cytoplasm, and intercellular bridges.
respiratory tract-Squamous cell carcinom.background_and_immunoprofile: Tumor diathesis with necrosis and keratin debris; p40, CK5/6, p63 positive; TTF-1/napsin A negative.
respiratory tract-Squamous cell carcinom.key_distinguishing_feature: Intercellular bridges and orangeophilic keratinization distinguish this vs Adenocarcinoma, which shows glandular clusters, mucin vacuoles, and TTF-1/napsin A.

respiratory tract-adenocarcinoma.organ_specimen: Respiratory tract, bronchial brushing or lung FNA specimen
respiratory tract-adenocarcinoma.diagnostic_code: PSC Category VI: Malignant — pulmonary adenocarcinoma
respiratory tract-adenocarcinoma.cytomorphology: Three-dimensional acinar, papillary, or glandular clusters show enlarged eccentric nuclei, irregular membranes, fine-to-coarse chromatin, prominent nucleoli, high N:C ratio, and vacuolated mucinous cytoplasm.
respiratory tract-adenocarcinoma.background_and_immunoprofile: Necrotic or mucinous background; TTF-1, napsin A, CK7 positive; p40/CK5/6 negative; molecular testing preserved.
respiratory tract-adenocarcinoma.key_distinguishing_feature: Intracytoplasmic mucin with glandular acini distinguishes this vs Squamous cell carcinoma, which has keratinization, bridges, and p40 positivity.

respiratory tract-Small cell carcinoma.organ_specimen: Respiratory tract, bronchial brushing or EBUS-FNA specimen
respiratory tract-Small cell carcinoma.diagnostic_code: PSC Category VI: Malignant — small cell carcinoma
respiratory tract-Small cell carcinoma.cytomorphology: Loose clusters and single small blue cells show nuclear molding, crush artifact, scant cytoplasm, very high N:C ratio, finely granular salt-and-pepper chromatin, absent nucleoli, and apoptotic bodies.
respiratory tract-Small cell carcinoma.background_and_immunoprofile: Necrosis, apoptotic debris and crush artifact; synaptophysin, chromogranin, CD56, TTF-1 positive; Ki-67 very high.
respiratory tract-Small cell carcinoma.key_distinguishing_feature: Nuclear molding with salt-and-pepper chromatin distinguishes this vs Lymphocyte, which lacks crush artifact and neuroendocrine marker expression.

Serous effusion-Breast cancer.organ_specimen: Serous cavity, pleural effusion or peritoneal fluid specimen
Serous effusion-Breast cancer.diagnostic_code: TIS Category V: MAL-S — metastatic breast ductal carcinoma
Serous effusion-Breast cancer.cytomorphology: Cohesive balls, morules, and single malignant cells show enlarged pleomorphic nuclei, irregular membranes, coarse chromatin, prominent nucleoli, high N:C ratio, and moderate vacuolated cytoplasm.
Serous effusion-Breast cancer.background_and_immunoprofile: Proteinaceous effusion with macrophages; GATA3, TRPS1, ER, mammaglobin/GCDFP-15 positive; calretinin/WT1 negative.
Serous effusion-Breast cancer.key_distinguishing_feature: GATA3/TRPS1-positive morules distinguish this vs Mesothelioma, which shows mesothelial windows and calretinin/D2-40 positivity.

Serous effusion-Ovarian cancer.organ_specimen: Serous cavity, peritoneal or pleural effusion specimen
Serous effusion-Ovarian cancer.diagnostic_code: TIS Category V: MAL-S — metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma
Serous effusion-Ovarian cancer.cytomorphology: Papillary fragments, spheroids, and single cells show marked pleomorphic nuclei, coarse chromatin, prominent nucleoli, high mitotic/apoptotic activity, high N:C ratio, and dense or vacuolated cytoplasm.
Serous effusion-Ovarian cancer.background_and_immunoprofile: Hemorrhagic proteinaceous effusion, psammoma bodies possible; PAX8, WT1, ER positive; p53 aberrant; calretinin negative.
Serous effusion-Ovarian cancer.key_distinguishing_feature: PAX8/WT1 positivity with aberrant p53 distinguishes this vs Reactive mesothelial proliferation, which retains calretinin and wild-type p53.

Serous effusion-adenocarcinoma.organ_specimen: Serous cavity, pleural/peritoneal/pericardial effusion cytology specimen
Serous effusion-adenocarcinoma.diagnostic_code: TIS Category V: MAL-S — metastatic adenocarcinoma NOS
Serous effusion-adenocarcinoma.cytomorphology: Tight three-dimensional clusters, acini, papillae, or signet-ring cells show enlarged irregular nuclei, coarse chromatin, prominent nucleoli, high N:C ratio, and mucin-vacuolated cytoplasm.
Serous effusion-adenocarcinoma.background_and_immunoprofile: Mucinous or necrotic effusion; claudin-4, MOC31, BerEP4, CEA positive; calretinin/WT1/D2-40 negative.
Serous effusion-adenocarcinoma.key_distinguishing_feature: Claudin-4-positive glandular clusters with mucin distinguish this vs Mesothelioma, which is claudin-4 negative and shows mesothelial windows.

Thyroid gland-MTC.organ_specimen: Thyroid gland, ultrasound-guided fine-needle aspiration specimen
Thyroid gland-MTC.diagnostic_code: Bethesda VI: Malignant — medullary thyroid carcinoma
Thyroid gland-MTC.cytomorphology: Discohesive plasmacytoid, spindle, or epithelioid cells show eccentric nuclei, coarse salt-and-pepper chromatin, occasional pseudoinclusions, variable N:C ratio, and granular amphophilic cytoplasm.
Thyroid gland-MTC.background_and_immunoprofile: Amyloid may be present; calcitonin, CEA, chromogranin, synaptophysin positive; thyroglobulin negative; RET testing relevant.
Thyroid gland-MTC.key_distinguishing_feature: Calcitonin-positive tumor with amyloid distinguishes this vs Follicular neoplasm, which expresses thyroglobulin and lacks neuroendocrine chromatin.

Thyroid gland-Suspicious for Malignancy.organ_specimen: Thyroid gland, ultrasound-guided fine-needle aspiration specimen
Thyroid gland-Suspicious for Malignancy.diagnostic_code: Bethesda V: Suspicious for malignancy — indeterminate malignant features
Thyroid gland-Suspicious for Malignancy.cytomorphology: Limited clusters or papillary/microfollicular fragments show focal malignant-type nuclear enlargement, irregular membranes, grooves or nucleoli, increased N:C ratio, and scant-to-moderate delicate cytoplasm.
Thyroid gland-Suspicious for Malignancy.background_and_immunoprofile: Scant colloid; CK19/HBME-1/galectin-3, calcitonin, or lymphoid markers selected by suspected tumor type.
Thyroid gland-Suspicious for Malignancy.key_distinguishing_feature: Strong but insufficient malignant features distinguish this vs Bethesda VI Malignant, which has unequivocal cytology and confirmatory lineage.

Thyroid gland-Malignant tumour.organ_specimen: Thyroid gland, ultrasound-guided FNA or cell-block specimen
Thyroid gland-Malignant tumour.diagnostic_code: Bethesda VI: Malignant — thyroid malignant tumor
Thyroid gland-Malignant tumour.cytomorphology: Cellular cohesive or discohesive malignant population shows marked nuclear enlargement, irregular membranes, coarse chromatin, prominent nucleoli or grooves, high N:C ratio, mitoses, and lineage-dependent cytoplasm.
Thyroid gland-Malignant tumour.background_and_immunoprofile: Necrosis or tumor matrix possible; PAX8/TTF-1/thyroglobulin, calcitonin, cytokeratin, or lymphoid markers classify lineage.
Thyroid gland-Malignant tumour.key_distinguishing_feature: Unequivocal malignant cytology with lineage marker support distinguishes this vs Bethesda V Suspicious, which is limited or qualitatively incomplete.
